chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings

text: 2363 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ نَتَفَ حَمَامَةً مِنْ حَمَامِ اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَالَ «يَتَصَدَّقُ بِصَدَقَةٍ عَلَى مِسْكِينٍ وَ يُعْطِي بِالْيَدِ اَلَّتِي نَتَفَ بِهَا فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ أَوْجَعَهُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2363 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Ibrahim ibn Maymun:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "A man plucked the feathers of a pigeon from the pigeons of the Haram."
Imam (as) replied: "He should give charity to a needy person and with the hand that plucked the feathers, for he has caused it pain."
